reasons: Diggie can reduce the value of Jawhead's pickoff plan by protecting allies during the throw-and-stun window.
Jawhead becomes less reliable when his first target does not stay controlled long enough for follow-up damage.

proof.summary: Jawhead wants to isolate one target with Ejector and follow-up control; Diggie can protect the target and lower the value of the pick.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves his protection for Jawhead's real engage.
Allies stay close enough to receive protection.
failureCases: Jawhead baits Diggie's protection before committing ultimate.

reasons: Valir's knockback and self-cleanse tools make it harder for Jawhead to finish direct engage.
Valir can zone Jawhead before Jawhead gets a clean throw angle.

proof.summary: Jawhead needs close access for Ejector; Valir can push him away and use defensive timing to deny or survive the engage.
worksBestWhen: Valir keeps distance and saves knockback for Jawhead's entry.
Valir does not waste his defensive ultimate before the real engage.
failureCases: Jawhead catches Valir from fog before he can react.
Jawhead targets another ally instead of Valir.

reasons: Kaja can punish Jawhead after Jawhead commits into the team.
Direct lockdown makes Jawhead's dive risky if he cannot instantly secure the pick.

proof.summary: Jawhead's engage is strongest during Ejector or ultimate timing; Kaja can hold lockdown for the moment Jawhead loses his safe entry window.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits until Jawhead commits rather than starting first.
The team bursts Jawhead during the lockdown.
failureCases: Jawhead's team follows faster than Kaja's team can punish.
Kaja is thrown before he can use lockdown.

reasons: Franco can punish Jawhead's predictable engage route with hook or lockdown.
If Jawhead is caught before Ejector timing, his pickoff pressure drops heavily.

proof.summary: Jawhead wants to choose the first target; Franco can reverse that plan by catching Jawhead before the throw setup.
worksBestWhen: Franco controls bush vision around turtle, lord, and mid lane.
Franco saves lockdown for Jawhead or the hero Jawhead wants to deliver.
failureCases: Franco misses hook and Jawhead immediately engages.
Jawhead uses minion or tank bodies to block hook threat.

reasons: Martis can contest Jawhead in close skirmishes and resist many control attempts during Mortal Coil.
His damage reduction and control immunity can make Jawhead's short burst window less reliable.

proof.summary: Jawhead wants short control windows, while Martis can use Mortal Coil's control immunity and damage reduction to survive or reverse close fights.
worksBestWhen: Martis times Mortal Coil into Jawhead's engage window.
Martis has nearby allies to punish Jawhead after the throw attempt.
failureCases: Martis uses Mortal Coil too early.
Jawhead isolates a different backline target instead of fighting Martis.
